question: What is the type of the given dataset.
keywords: type format datatype image text audio video multimodal tabular structured unstructured
summary: Describes the type of the given dataset.
description:
Describes the datatype contained in the dataset.

For example, a dataset can be an image dataset for computer vision applications, a text dataset such as the contents of a book or Wikipedia article, or sometimes a multimodal dataset that contains multiple types of data.

retrieval.hypothetical_passage: This is a multimodal vision-language dataset pairing 1.2M images with English captions. Images are RGB JPEGs at 224x224, and captions are stored as UTF-8 text averaging 14 tokens. A small auxiliary split contains 50k audio clips with aligned transcripts for ASR experiments. Tabular metadata (timestamp, geolocation, license) accompanies each row. The dataset complements text-only corpora such as C4 and is suited to image-text retrieval and captioning benchmarks.

extraction.instruction: Extract the data modality (or modalities) of the dataset, mapping each to the SPDX DatasetType vocabulary.
extraction.field_spec: SPDX property `dataset_datasetType`. Enum DatasetType, 1..* values; members: `audio`, `categorical`, `graph`, `image`, `no-assertion`, `numeric`, `other`, `sensor`, `structured`, `syntactic`, `text`, `timeseries`, `timestamp`, `video`. Format: comma-separated enum tokens.
extraction.output_guidance: Use multiple enum tokens for multimodal datasets (e.g., 'image, text'). Map vocabulary: 'corpus/captions/transcripts' -> text; 'photos/frames' -> image; 'tables/CSV/Parquet' -> structured; 'numeric features' -> numeric; 'class labels' -> categorical. If the modality is unclear, return `no-assertion`. Do not invent modalities not evidenced in the source. Return only the bare enum tokens with no explanatory prose.
